If you’re setting up network storage in your home, you may want an extra multi-gig Ethernet port that can be used for LAN.

Find a router’s actual speed

The speed of a router is often presented as the Wi-Fi prefix, like AX, followed by a number, such as AX5400. The prefix for Wi-Fi 5 is AC, Wi-Fi 6 is AX, and Wi-Fi 7 is BE. Wi-Fi 6E is written either as AX or AXE depending on the manufacturer. These are aggregate speeds that combine all of the top speeds across all bands together.

Some listings are happy to promote an AX5400 router as being capable of 5.4Gbps, but it’s not quite that simple. For example, most AX5400 routers offer 574Mbps to devices on the 2.4GHz band and 4804Mbps on the 5GHz band. Since most Wi-Fi 6 devices support connections up to 1201Mbps with some offering 2402Mbps, you’ll never actually see 5.4Gbps on any one device. Not to mention that a lot of these routers don’t even support wired connections that fast.

If you want the best speed out of a fast router, you’ll need a Wi-Fi card, like the Asus PBE -whatever it is, with support for 160MHz connections. That enables connection speeds up to 2402Mbps on Wi-Fi 6 and even higher with Wi-Fi 7. Wi-Fi 7 also supports up to 320MHz bands at 6GHz, though client support is still, and will likely remain, mostly 160MHz.

MU-MIMO stands for multiple user, multiple input, multiple output, and is used to connect multiple devices at once to Wi-Fi. This helps improve average speeds and keep connections responsive. MU-MIMO is supported by most mid-range and higher Wi-Fi 6 routers and is key to achieving their top speeds. If you’ve got more than a handful of Wi-Fi devices in your home, MU-MIMO is a worthwhile addition.

OFDMA and QAM are two technologies that help keep Wi-fi speeds high, even when there's a lot of congestion. Many Wi-Fi 6 routers offer 1024-QAM, but some go with smaller numbers for budget models. If you've got a lot of wireless congestion around you, such as in an apartment building, going for the full 1024-QAM, or 4K-QAM on Wi-Fi 7, could offer some speed benefits.

Security

When you enter a password to connect to Wi-Fi, that password needs to be sent to the router somehow to allow the connection. The trick is sending that password in a way that can’t be observed. Older security protocols like WEP weren’t very secure at all, and with the right software, the password could be obtained rather quickly. WPA2 is a significant improvement over that, and WAP3 is another improvement on top of that. A router with WPA3 support can be more secure than one with just WPA2, though your devices will also need to support it to fully switch over.

Continuing with security, many people prefer to browse the web with a VPN adding an extra layer of security to their connection. A lot of modern routers support using a VPN for your devices. VPN Server allows you to access your home network from an outside connection, while VPN Client allows you to add one of the best VPN providers to your router. If you want to use a VPN with your router, check that it supports OpenVPN, and maybe even Wireguard for better speeds.

Related
How to install a VPN on your router
If you want to protect your whole home network, installing a VPN directly on your router is the most convenient route.

Also, keep in mind that you’ll want a faster CPU to handle that VPN connection. This all depends on how many devices you’re connecting, but if you plan on using a VPN for your whole home, getting a router with a fast CPU can go a long way.

App and account requirement

Some routers require you to use an app to set up, and some people don’t like that. Setup and management apps can be very convenient for getting up and running quickly, but often require you to create an online account, and may try to upsell you on security or parental control subscriptions. From a privacy perspective, some of these apps also try to get you to share some usage information, which not everyone is a fan of. Check the router’s requirements to see if you have to use the app and create an online account.

If there is an online account, there’s a good chance there are also subscription services available on the router. These can be security add-ons like TP-Link HomeShield, Netgear Armor, or Eero Secure. Parental controls may also be available, like Netgear’s Smart Parental Controls or Gryphon Premium Protection. They all offer different levels of free and paid features, so you’ll want to see which works best for you. Some brands offer this type of feature for free, like Asus and Acer, though your mileage may vary with each one. A lot of people just skip these add-ons entirely, so if you’re not feeling them, you’re not alone.

Mesh systems like Nest Wifi, Eero, and Deco only allow customers to change the most basic settings, so if you want to get into the advanced settings, make sure your router allows access.

Mesh compatibility

Mesh compatibility is great for adding coverage to your home without needing to replace your base router. Simply put, other routers are used as Wi-Fi repeaters to improve your coverage. Unlike a traditional repeater though, mesh Wi-Fi uses the same Wi-Fi name and your device can automatically roam between the different nodes in your mesh.

EasyMesh can be used by just about any router maker and is quickly gaining support across the industry, but there are still a lot of proprietary solutions to look out for. AiMesh from Asus works on just about any Asus router, for example. Others like Deco, Eero, and Nest Wifi work with other mesh routers from their family. If you think you’ll need a mesh in the future, make sure you’re buying into a platform with plenty of expansion options for your needs.

Gaming routers

If you’re a gamer looking for a good router that can keep up with your streaming and gaming needs, you mostly just need a fast one. Gaming routers can have more advanced QoS (quality of service) software to prioritize data, and may even have built-in NAT configurations for popular games. For gaming, a fast CPU can help keep your ping times consistent if you’ve got a lot of devices on your network. Furthermore, faster Wi-Fi can give you some headroom to keep speeds high for long periods.

Most gaming routers include a QoS to keep gaming traffic first in line so ping times remain as low as possible. On a shared connection, especially a slower connection, a QoS can allow high-priority traffic to cut in line to keep ping times low. To someone in your home streaming video or browsing the web, a few extra milliseconds likely won’t even be noticed, while they can have a major impact in games.

Related
What is QoS on routers?
QoS on your router keeps your most important connections feeling fast by prioritizing their packets.

That being said, if at all possible, you should be using a wired connection for gaming. Running one of the best Ethernet cables from your router to your gaming PC is one of the most effective ways to improve your gaming connection.

Be cautious about coverage numbers

While router specs are essential to making sure you’re getting the right product, they can also create unrealistic expectations in consumers. One of the biggest problem specs is coverage. While many routers have removed this spec entirely, many mesh systems still boast about coverage in square feet.

Antenna design has continued to improve in routers over the past few years, but there’s no arguing with physics. If you’ve got a large home, a router or mesh kit will very likely not live up to the advertised coverage area. The problem is that all of our homes are different and everything from home appliances to the materials our walls are made of can greatly impact coverage.

Most people don’t need all that much speed

While it's easy to get caught up in all the tech specs on a product like a router, the fact of the matter is that most people will be perfectly happy with a mid to low-end Wi-Fi 6 router. While it's fun to have a top-of-the-line multi-gig router, most of our internet connections aren’t that fast and don’t need to be. While the speed test results weren’t all that impressive we found using a cheaper router like the AX3000 Asus RT-AX57 offered plenty of speed for streaming, browsing, working, and even gaming. Even the AX1800 EnGenius ECW220 performed well enough to earn a recommendation with everyday computing and streaming.

Before blowing a ton of money on a router, take a step back and think about how much speed you really need. While incredibly fast routers like the TP-Link Archer BE800 offer excellent user experiences, the fact of the matter is that most people don’t need that much speed. If you focus on the specs you need most, you can save a lot of money while still getting one of the best Wi-Fi routers.

item.title: How to backup all your data on an Android smartphone
item.itunes:title: How to backup all your data on an Android smartphone
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Back up your Android phone using Google One
  • Backup your photos and videos with Google Photos
  • Create cloud save using Google Drive or other-party apps like Dropbox
  • Using NANDroid backups in a custom recovery environment
  • Using third-party backup apps on Android
  • Connecting your Android phone to a Windows PC or a Mac
  • Using an external drive for manual backups

It's never a bad time to back up your smartphone because there's always a possibility that you may break it, drop it in water, or even misplace it and lose all your data. You may even be getting ready to upgrade to one of the best new phones and need to make sure your data is secure. Whatever the case is, you wouldn't want to lose or let go of all your photos, important documents, or contacts. That's why I can't stress enough that it's important to back up all your data frequently. While you can back up all your data manually, there are plenty of ways you can automate the process to ensure you never lose your precious data.

Related
Best Android phones in 2024
Need a new phone? Look no further than this selection of best Android phones you should buy, including the best from Google, Samsung and more!

Back up your Android phone using Google One

A simple and straightforward way to back up

Source: Google

The easiest way to back up the data stored on your Android smartphone is with Google One. It allows for simple and seamless backups, and all you have to do is download the Google One app from the Play Store to perform a one-time setup.

Google One can save your app data, chat and call history, device settings, and Google Account data, including contacts, calendars, Gmail, and more. Notably, Google One will also sync your photos and videos with your Google Photos library at your preferred quality. The best thing about using it is that it can automatically back up your data over Wi-Fi after the device has been idle and charging for two hours. All Google account holders also get 15GB of free cloud storage for device backups, which is plenty for casual users. More advanced users can purchase an additional 100GB of storage for as little as $2 per month, so it's still a great way to save all your backups without spending a lot of money.

Here's how to get started with Google One to create your first backup and setup automatic backups over Wi-Fi:

1. Download and open the Google One app on your phone.

2. Select the Setup backup button and then choose Turn On to enable Google One backups on your phone.

3. Now, you can decide what data you want to back up to Google One, including device data, messages, and even photos and videos.

4. Tap Back up now once you've confirmed your selection to begin backing up your device data to Google One.

Once the backup is finished, you can easily access your backed-up data from within the app. Here's how to access it:

1. Go to the home page of the Google One app and select the Storage tab at the bottom to see how much storage you have left, what's taking up the storage space, and more.

2. Scroll down to locate your last backup, and select it to expand the preview card with a Show details button.

3. Select that to check your backup details along with a Back up now button in case you want to immediately run another backup.

Backup your photos and videos with Google Photos

Don't leave any of your memories behind

Google One automatically syncs your photos and videos to your Google Photos library, so you don't have to repeat the process if you've opted to sync media during your initial Google One backup. But if you can only care about saving your photos and videos, then you can simply use the backup option within the Google Photos app to secure your media. Here's how to do it:

1. Download and open the Google Photos app on your phone, and log in to your account.

2. You'll see a prompt asking you to turn on backup if you're launching it for the first time. If not, select the profile icon located in the top right corner and tap Turn on backup.

3. Google Photos will now start backing up the photos and videos you have on your phone. You can check the status by tapping your profile icon once again.

4. It'll show a Backup Complete message once it's done, and you should be good to go.

Create cloud save using Google Drive or other-party apps like Dropbox

Manually send the data to the cloud

If you'd rather have manual control over which files and documents you back up and how you organize them on the cloud, then you might want to consider using Google Drive or third-party services like Dropbox. You can download and use these applications on your Android phone to upload pretty much any file, including media, manually to a preferred location on the cloud and organize them. You can create additional folders to move around your files and even view them anytime via mobile or desktop. The process for that is as simple as selecting the files you want to back up and uploading them to the cloud.

Google Drive also has a built-in Backup and restore option, but that'll navigate you back to your device backup setting, which is essentially what we achieved earlier in this tutorial using Google One. I suggest you ignore that option and simply upload the files you want to back up manually.

Similarly, you can also use Microsoft OneDrive to sync your files between a mobile and the desktop with just a few taps. OneDrive is Microsoft's official online storage that's used by default on Windows computers. You can download the OneDrive app on your Android phone to seamlessly share, back up, and even view files between the two devices. You get 5GB of free cloud storage on OneDrive, but you can always upgrade your account to add more space.

Using NANDroid backups in a custom recovery environment

For advanced users

This one's for all the enthusiasts and tinkerers out there. If you have a phone with an unlocked bootloader and a custom recovery like TWRP, you can take a complete backup of your system and data from the recovery partitions. This method, known as NANDroid backup in the modding community, creates a complete copy of your device and restores it to its previous state when needed. It essentially lets you save and restore every aspect of your phone.

Custom recovery backups are a lifesaver when trying out bleeding edge custom ROMs and mods. If something goes wrong, and you end up with a soft-bricked device, you can simply boot into recovery and restore your backup. It will bring everything back to life and restore precisely how you left it.

Using third-party backup apps on Android

Feel free to pick the one you like

There's no shortage of third-party apps on the Google Play Store, so you'll come across endless applications to get the job done.

Swift Backup

I've used Swift Backup in the past to restore my data, so that's what I'd recommend. Swift Backup lets you perform batch actions to let you back up all your applications, messages, and call logs separately. You can even connect a cloud account to Swift Backup to bring all your backups to one place. For instance, you can connect to Google Drive to back up or restore the files to/from the Drive. It's very simple to use, and you'll get started with your backups with Swift Backup in no time.

Titanium Backup

Titanium Backup is also an excellent option, and it gets recommended a lot in our XDA forums among enthusiasts. This app lets you back up all your apps, including system apps with data, which can save you a lot of time when dealing with hundreds of apps. However, you need root access to be able to use all those features in the Titanium Backup app, so keep that in mind. It is a great tool overall for when you're switching ROMs and want to restore all your apps and data with a single press of a button.

Connecting your Android phone to a Windows PC or a Mac

Back up the data directly to your PC

Another way to create a backup of the files stored on your Android device is by connecting it to a Windows PC or Mac. Those using a Windows laptop or a desktop can use a beta app for Windows called Nearby Share to wirelessly connect their Windows PC and Android phone for file transfers. Using Nearby Share is different from using OneDrive because this is designed to send files over a wireless connection to nearby devices only, whereas OneDrive is a cloud solution that doesn't need the devices to be close to each other or on the same wireless network.

Source: Android

Mac users, on the other hand, can use Android File Transfer to connect their Android phone to a Mac computer. This is the best tool you can use to create a local backup of your Android phone on a Mac computer, and I suggest you head over to our How to transfer files from Android to Mac guide to learn more.

Using an external drive for manual backups

A tried-and-tested solution that never fails

Lastly, you can choose to ditch all the online cloud-based solutions and wireless methods in favor of offline backups using a computer or an external storage device. This particular method is not for everyone, as it needs a separate computer or a storage device to get the job done. It is, however, a reliable way to back up your Android phone.

The process involves physically connecting your Android phone to a computer or a storage device via a USB cable to transfer your files one by one. You can transfer pretty much everything, including music, photos, documents, and more. You can also use Swift Backup to create a copy of all your data locally on your phone and then copy them to the computer or your storage device.

Plenty of ways to back up your Android phone in 2024

Gone are the days when you had to create multiple backups using various apps and services. Google One can handle everything with just a few taps, and it also supports automatic backup features. I also recommend checking out third-party backup apps like Swift Backup, which also lets you handle pretty much everything from one place with batch actions. I've also highlighted some ways in which you can create manual backups of the files stored on your Android device, so be sure to check them all out. It's all about finding the best method that works for you, so feel free to experiment with different services before settling.

Similarly, you can also use Microsoft OneDrive to sync your files between a mobile and the desktop with just a few taps. OneDrive is Microsoft's official online storage that's used by default on Windows computers. You can download the OneDrive app on your Android phone to seamlessly share, back up, and even view files between the two devices. You get 5GB of free cloud storage on OneDrive, but you can always upgrade your account to add more space.

Using NANDroid backups in a custom recovery environment

For advanced users

This one's for all the enthusiasts and tinkerers out there. If you have a phone with an unlocked bootloader and a custom recovery like TWRP, you can take a complete backup of your system and data from the recovery partitions. This method, known as NANDroid backup in the modding community, creates a complete copy of your device and restores it to its previous state when needed. It essentially lets you save and restore every aspect of your phone.

Custom recovery backups are a lifesaver when trying out bleeding edge custom ROMs and mods. If something goes wrong, and you end up with a soft-bricked device, you can simply boot into recovery and restore your backup. It will bring everything back to life and restore precisely how you left it.

Using third-party backup apps on Android

Feel free to pick the one you like

There's no shortage of third-party apps on the Google Play Store, so you'll come across endless applications to get the job done.

Swift Backup

I've used Swift Backup in the past to restore my data, so that's what I'd recommend. Swift Backup lets you perform batch actions to let you back up all your applications, messages, and call logs separately. You can even connect a cloud account to Swift Backup to bring all your backups to one place. For instance, you can connect to Google Drive to back up or restore the files to/from the Drive. It's very simple to use, and you'll get started with your backups with Swift Backup in no time.

Titanium Backup

Titanium Backup is also an excellent option, and it gets recommended a lot in our XDA forums among enthusiasts. This app lets you back up all your apps, including system apps with data, which can save you a lot of time when dealing with hundreds of apps. However, you need root access to be able to use all those features in the Titanium Backup app, so keep that in mind. It is a great tool overall for when you're switching ROMs and want to restore all your apps and data with a single press of a button.

Connecting your Android phone to a Windows PC or a Mac

Back up the data directly to your PC

Another way to create a backup of the files stored on your Android device is by connecting it to a Windows PC or Mac. Those using a Windows laptop or a desktop can use a beta app for Windows called Nearby Share to wirelessly connect their Windows PC and Android phone for file transfers. Using Nearby Share is different from using OneDrive because this is designed to send files over a wireless connection to nearby devices only, whereas OneDrive is a cloud solution that doesn't need the devices to be close to each other or on the same wireless network.

Source: Android

Mac users, on the other hand, can use Android File Transfer to connect their Android phone to a Mac computer. This is the best tool you can use to create a local backup of your Android phone on a Mac computer, and I suggest you head over to our How to transfer files from Android to Mac guide to learn more.

Using an external drive for manual backups

A tried-and-tested solution that never fails

Lastly, you can choose to ditch all the online cloud-based solutions and wireless methods in favor of offline backups using a computer or an external storage device. This particular method is not for everyone, as it needs a separate computer or a storage device to get the job done. It is, however, a reliable way to back up your Android phone.

The process involves physically connecting your Android phone to a computer or a storage device via a USB cable to transfer your files one by one. You can transfer pretty much everything, including music, photos, documents, and more. You can also use Swift Backup to create a copy of all your data locally on your phone and then copy them to the computer or your storage device.

Plenty of ways to back up your Android phone in 2024

Gone are the days when you had to create multiple backups using various apps and services. Google One can handle everything with just a few taps, and it also supports automatic backup features. I also recommend checking out third-party backup apps like Swift Backup, which also lets you handle pretty much everything from one place with batch actions. I've also highlighted some ways in which you can create manual backups of the files stored on your Android device, so be sure to check them all out. It's all about finding the best method that works for you, so feel free to experiment with different services before settling.

item.title: Best 16-inch laptops in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best 16-inch laptops in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
For many years, 15-inch laptops were some of the most popular on the market since they offered a nice balance of portability and screen real estate. But in recent years, you may have noticed a trend where more and more 16-inch laptops replace 15-inch models. Many of the best laptops you can buy today have 16-inch screens, and fewer have 15-inch displays.

The reason for that is simple — we're not just making screens bigger. In most cases, 16-inch laptops come in a 15-inch form factor, and that's because companies are moving away from the classic 16:9 aspect ratio that became the standard in the mid-2000s. Instead, we now have a taller 16:10 format, which is far better for productivity. The taller screen means you get more vertical space to hold more lines of text, for a better view of the video and audio tracks in a video editing interface, and to more easily work with all the clutch tools in a photo editor like Photoshop.

There are lots of great reasons to get a 16-inch laptop compared to a 15-inch one, so we're here to help you choose the best one for yourself. There's no shortage of solid options on the market at this point, so let's dive right in.

  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best overall
    $1450 at Samsung
  • Source: HP
    HP Spectre x360 16 (2024)
    Best convertible
    $1300 at HP
  • Apple MacBook Pro (M2, 2023)
    Best creator laptop
    $1999 at Amazon (16 inches)
  • Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
    Best business laptop
    $1391 at Lenovo
  • Acer Chromebook 516 GE
    Best Chromebook
    $449 at Best Buy
  • Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
    Best gaming laptop
    $2049 at B&H
  • LG Gram 16 (2023)
    Best lightweight laptop
    $1030 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best budget laptop
    $550 at Lenovo
Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall

Fast performance, stunning display, and premium build

$1663 $1750 Save $87

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core processors deliver great performance
  • Beautiful Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Good supply of ports
Cons
  • HDMI output limited to 4K at 30Hz
  • No Windows Hello facial recognition
$1450 at Samsung $1663 at Best Buy

Samsung has been making some pretty great laptops over the past few years, then in 2023, it became one of the best in the industry with the Galaxy Book 3 Pro.

In terms of performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel processors, up to a Core i7-1260P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which deliver great performance for day-to-day tasks. It's a mild but welcome upgrade over the previous generation, and with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD, you are equipped for a great experience.

The big upgrade is the display. The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is the first in the lineup to come in a 16-inch size thanks to switching to the 16:10 aspect ratio. The resolution is much sharper, too, at 2880x1800, and it even has a smooth 120Hz refresh rate now. It's simply better in every way. The webcam has the same 1080p sensor, and although there's no facial recognition, you can use Windows Hello with the fingerprint reader on the power button.

Another notable upgrade is the design, with Samsung switching to an aluminum chassis that feels much more premium and sturdy. It looks sleek and subdued in both Graphite and Beige colorways, too. Unfortunately, aluminum is much heavier than magnesium, so this laptop comes in at 3.44 pounds, even though it's still very thin. It's still portable, but significantly heavier than its predecessor. However, the port selection here is great, including two Thunderbolt 4 ports, one USB Type-A, HDMI, a 3.5mm headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

Previous Samsung Galaxy laptops were already great, but the changes made to the Galaxy Book 3 Pro make this one of the best laptops on the market.

HP Spectre x360 16 (2024)
Best convertible
$1300 $1600 Save $300

The 2024 HP Spectre x360 16 comes with powerful Intel processors and optional Nvidia RTX 4050 graphics for workloads that require more GPU power. It has a super-sharp 16-inch display, including an optional OLED panel, too.

Pros
  • Intel Core Ultra chip
  • OLED panel option
  • Dedicated GPU available
Cons
  • Relatively bulky
$1300 at HP $1750 at Best Buy

Clamshell laptops are fine, but if you're like me, nothing beats the versatility of a convertible. If you're looking for a large one, the HP Spectre x360 16 is your best bet.

The latest model of the HP Spectre x360 16 comes with an Intel Core Ultra 7 155H chip featuring 16 cores and 22 threads. Graphics-wise, you can choose between Intel's Arc integrated solution or opt for an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050. Naturally, the 4050 has more performance than the integrated Arc graphics, which will be the obvious pick for creators that need more GPU power. You can also get this laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

2:25
Related
HP Spectre x360 16 (2024) review: Major changes pay off for HP's premium convertible
A 9MP webcam, improved keyboard, new speaker setup, and massive haptic touchpad are just some of the standout features.

When it comes to display, you can choose between a 2560x1600 120Hz IPS panel or a 2.8K 120Hz OLED panel. Thanks to the more premium panel tech with OLED, you will get richer image quality as well as a higher peak brightness of 500 nits vs the IPS panel's 400 nits. You're also getting a truly impressive 9MP webcam with infared capability, human presence detection, Windows hello, and privacy alerts.

In terms of looks, the Spectre x360 16 comes in a unique dual-tone color scheme, mixing black with brass accents, or dark blue with light blue accents. Being a large convertible, it's a fairly heavy laptop, coming in at 4.3 pounds. Still, it's decently portable. As for the ports, HP gives you two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, and a headphone jack. It's a pretty solid setup for a modern laptop.

The HP Spectre x360 16 can be a bit pricey, but it's a fantastic laptop in just about every way.

Apple MacBook Pro (M2, 2023)
Best creator laptop

Terrific performance and battery life

$1999 $2499 Save $500

The 2023 MacBook Pro builds on the 2021 model to deliver even more performance and efficiency while maintaining a top-tier display and build quality. It's one of the best laptops on the market, plain and simple.

Pros
  • Unrivaled performance and battery life thanks to the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max chips
  • Mini-LED display with 120Hz refresh rate and up to 1,600 nits of brightness
  • Premium build quality with an all-aluminum chassis
Cons
  • It may look a bit boring to some
  • Not everyone wants to use macOS
  • It gets quite expensive
$1999 at Amazon (16 inches)

It's impossible to talk about the best laptops on the market without mentioning the MacBook Pro, especially if we're talking about 16-inch laptops. The latest model is a phenomenal machine for performance and battery life, making it easily the best laptop for creators.

Indeed, the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max are top-of-the-line processors with 12 CPU cores (eight for performance, four for efficiency) and up to a 39-core GPU that delivers a balance of performance and battery life that you just can't get anywhere. The performance can rival the best laptops in the Windows space, but it's so efficient that battery life is in a whole different class compared to its competitors. You can also get up to 96GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

To go along with the powerful internals, there's also an absolutely fantastic screen. It's a 16.2-inch panel, and it comes in a super-sharp 3456x2234 resolution, resulting in an aspect ratio slightly taller than 16:10. The screen has a 120Hz adaptive refresh rate that makes motion and animations look smoother. Plus, it has a Mini-LED display with hundreds of individual dimming zones, and it can reach a maximum brightness of 1,600 nits. The only thing you might not like is the notch in the middle of the screen, which is where you'll find the 1080p webcam that delivers very good image quality.

Design-wise, the MacBook Pro isn't particularly exciting, but it is premium. It's a solid aluminum laptop that comes in either Silver or Space Grey (dark silver), so it looks very subdued. Thanks to the powerful specs, it gets a bit heavy, starting at 4.7 pounds of weight. Finally, in terms of ports, the latest MacBook Pro models come with three Thunderbolt 4 ports, along with HDMI, a headphone jack, a full-size SD card reader, and a MagSafe connector for charging. It's worth noting that the Thunderbolt ports don't support things like external GPUs here, but otherwise, it's a solid setup.

This is undoubtedly one of the best laptops on the market, and while it's pricey, we couldn't not recommend it.

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

Subdued design and multiple configurations available

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is a refreshed version of the ThinkPad X1. It now features the more powerful 13th-gen Intel processors, while keeping the class ThinkPad Design we all know and love. It's one of the most powerful business laptops on the market, and it delivers the reliability Lenovo has become known for.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel U- or P-series processors
  • Subdued design with a lot of ports
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • Somewhat heavy
  • Expensive, especially for build-to-order configurations
$1391 at Lenovo $2050 at Best Buy

If you need a laptop for work, and you want one with a 16-inch display to get more done faster, the ThinkPad X1 Carbon is a great option that checks all the boxes without being overly fancy.

At a performance level, you can choose 13th-generation Intel processors from either the P-series or the U-series. The P-series models go up to a Core i7-1370P with 14 cores and 20 threads, while the U-series maxes out with a Core i7-1365U with 10 cores and 12 threads. P-series processors can be faster, while the U series is better for battery life. Additionally, you can get up to 64GB of RAM (user-removable) and a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD.

You'll get a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and since this is a laptop focused on nailing the basics, the entry-level configuration comes in Full HD+ resolution and 400 nits of brightness. However, you can also opt for a 2.8K OLED panel for a stunning visual experience with 500 nits (HDR) of peak brightness. Above that display is also a 5MP webcam with optional Windows Hello support.

For a business laptop, the Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon is exactly what you'd expect. It features the design we all know and love from the ThinkPad, with a carbon fiber top and an aluminum bottom. Starting at 2.4 pounds, it's a comfortable laptop to take with you to the office and beyond. It also features great connectivity, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and even an optional Smart Card reader.

Acer Chromebook 516 GE
Best Chromebook

Fast performance and a sharp display for ChromeOS

$449 $649 Save $200

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE is marketed as a gaming laptop, but it's just a great laptop for anyone. With 12th-generation Intel Core processors and a sharp display, it's perfect for all kinds of users, plus it has some personality with the RGB keyboard.

Pros
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • RGB keyboard adds some personality
Cons
  • Windows laptops have newer processors now
  • No biometric authentication
$530 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy

There isn't a huge market for 16-inch Chromebooks out there, but that shouldn't take away from how good the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is. If you want the best ChromeOS laptop on the market, this should be on your mind regardless of size.

Powering the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is a 12th-generation Intel Core i5-1240P processor with 12 cores and 16 threads. It's not the newest processor around, but it's still very fast, especially on ChromeOS. It comes with a pretty modest 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD for storage. It may be advertised for gaming, but those are specs you'd find in any ultrabook, which may actually be a good thing since it makes this a great machine for anyone.

What makes it even better is the 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio and sharp Quad HD+ resolution. That creates a great viewing experience, and it's excellent for getting work done. It even has a 120Hz refresh rate (where some of that gaming DNA comes in), so animations and motion look much smoother. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, though there's no biometric authentication available there.

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE doesn't try to be too fancy in terms of looks, with a simple black design, but it does have an RGB keyboard which adds a bit of flair if you're into that. At 3.75 pounds, it's not exactly light, but it's reasonable for the size. Ports are pretty good, too, with two USB Type-C and one Type-A port, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack.

As far as Chromebooks go, it doesn't get much better than the Chromebook 516 GE, so if you want one in the 16-inch size, you can't go wrong here.

Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
Best gaming laptop

Monster performance in a premium design

$2049 $2749 Save $700

The Lenovo Legion Pro 7i is a beastly gaming laptop with top-notch performance. It has incredibly powerful Intel processors and Nvidia graphics, along with a great display for gaming. It even has a premium metal design.

Pros
  • Up to a 24-core processor and Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics
  • Quad HD+ display with super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate
  • Lots of ports
Cons
  • No Windows Hello support
  • Heavy
  • Expensive
$2990 at Lenovo $2049 at B&H

Gaming is one of the most popular hobbies in the world, and the 16-inch form factor is arguably ideal for it. If you want the best option to support that activity, the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i should definitely be on your radar.

Performance is one thing that this laptop certainly isn't missing, with up to a 24-core, 32-thread Intel Core i9-13900HX delivering absolutely outstanding performance for a laptop. That, combined with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, gives you all the performance you would want to run your games at high settings and high frame rates. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD to store all of your games.

You're getting a great display here too: a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio and super-sharp Quad HD+ resolution, making it easy to get some work done too. And with the smooth 240Hz refresh rate, gaming will be as enjoyable as it gets. Most games probably can't hit that maximum resolution and refresh rate, but for lighter titles, this is fantastic. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, which is nice to see, although there's no Windows Hello support (not even through a fingerprint reader).

The Legion Pro 7i also impresses in terms of design, since it's a mostly metal chassis that looks and feels very premium. Combined with the high-end specs, this makes for a very heavy laptop, starting at 6.17 pounds, but that makes sense with something like this. And in terms of ports, we're looking at two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), four USB Type-A ports, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack, so there's no shortage of connectivity options.

There's a lot to love in the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i, and if you can afford it, it's one of the very best gaming laptops on the market.

LG Gram 16 (2023)
Best lightweight laptop

Fast performance in a portable package

The LG Gram 16 is a lightweight and powerful 16-inch laptop that features a crisp display, solid battery life, and a very sleek design. Featuring Intel's 13th-generation CPUs, there's enough power for this to be your ultra portable work machine.

Pros
  • Incredibly sleek design
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display
  • Just 2.6 pounds
Cons
  • Lack of a touchscreen option
$1030 at Amazon

Bigger laptops do tend to be heavier (naturally), but if you want a machine that has a bigger screen, impressive performance, and is also super light, then the LG Gram 16 is for you.

First off, in terms of performance, the LG Gram 16 comes with up to a 13th-generation Intel Core i7, with 12 cores and 16 threads, able to handle day-to-day tasks without a hitch. You can choose between 16GB or 32GB of RAM, and you also get a 1TB SSD. This leaves no room for poor performance here, which explains the higher price tag.

By default, the LG Gram 16 comes with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics, but you have the option of upgrading to a model with a discrete Nvidia RTX 3050 for an extra boost to the graphics performance. Adding to that is a crisp IPS panel that delivers Quad HD+ resolution. The configuration with the discrete RTX 3050 will offer a variable refresh rate of up to 144Hz, but the standard Gram 16 with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics comes with a standard 60Hz IPS panel. You also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition support. The best part: all of these things are included by default.

Finally, there's the design, with the first highlight being the fact that this 16-inch laptop starts at just 2.62 pounds for the configuration with integrated graphics. The dedicated GPU factors into the weight though, bringing the total weight of the LG Gram 16 to 2.86 pounds from 2.62 pounds. There are slightly lighter 16-inch laptops out there, but this one makes up for it in performance capacity. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, so it's a solid setup.

Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
Best budget laptop

A solid experience on the cheap

$550 $750 Save $200

The Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a relatively basic laptop, but it covers the basics with solid performance and it has a 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio. Plus, since it's a convertible, it supports touch and pen input, too.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors
  • Touchscreen with pen support and a 16:10 aspect ratio
  • 1080p webcam
Cons
  • Fairly heavy
  • Display doesn't have the best colors
$550 at Lenovo

Not everyone wants to spend thousands of dollars on a laptop, but the 16-inch form factor does tend to be reserved for more premium products. Thankfully, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 convertible is an exception, which does enough to offer you a good experience for day-to-day use.

The base model of the IdeaPad Flex 5 comes with an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, a solid processor with 6 cores and 12 threads, which should be more than enough for web browsing, watching movies, and creating documents or presentations. It starts at 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD at minimum, with some upgrade options if you want to spend a little more.

Having a 16-inch display in this price range is fairly rare, but it's great to see one with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution, even if the colors won't look quite as amazing as the others here. Plus, since this is a convertible, you can use touch input and even a pen (included in some models) to interact with it more naturally. To top it off, there's even a 1080p webcam, which lacks Windows Hello support, but you can use the fingerprint reader for that instead.

Design is one area that tends to be lacking in budget laptops, and here, it kind of shows. This one uses a mix of aluminum and plastic for the build, which makes for a heavy laptop starting at 4.63 pounds. Again, not unexpected for something in this price range. It does come in two color options, though both are fairly subdued. As for ports, one USB-C connection, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader make for a pretty good experience overall.

With the regular price starting at $700, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great laptop with a versatile form factor at a very reasonable price. If you want to save money in this category, this is the way to go.

Best 16-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

These options cover a lot of use cases and there's something for everyone here. If you want the best overall experience, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is hard to beat. It has fast processors, a fantastic display, a premium design, and even a solid supply of ports that beats many competitors in the same space. It's a huge leap forward for the series too, and it's good to see Samsung invest heavily in making a premium device.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall
$1663 $1750 Save $87

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

$1450 at Samsung $1663 at Best Buy

If you aren't looking for something expensive, you can still have a great time with the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5. It doesn't have the amazing display or the same level of performance, but for day-to-day use at home or at school, it will meet your needs. It has a large screen, complete with touch support, and modern AMD processors that should deliver strong performance overall.

When it comes to display, you can choose between a 2560x1600 120Hz IPS panel or a 2.8K 120Hz OLED panel. Thanks to the more premium panel tech with OLED, you will get richer image quality as well as a higher peak brightness of 500 nits vs the IPS panel's 400 nits. You're also getting a truly impressive 9MP webcam with infared capability, human presence detection, Windows hello, and privacy alerts.

In terms of looks, the Spectre x360 16 comes in a unique dual-tone color scheme, mixing black with brass accents, or dark blue with light blue accents. Being a large convertible, it's a fairly heavy laptop, coming in at 4.3 pounds. Still, it's decently portable. As for the ports, HP gives you two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, and a headphone jack. It's a pretty solid setup for a modern laptop.

The HP Spectre x360 16 can be a bit pricey, but it's a fantastic laptop in just about every way.

Apple MacBook Pro (M2, 2023)
Best creator laptop

Terrific performance and battery life

$1999 $2499 Save $500

The 2023 MacBook Pro builds on the 2021 model to deliver even more performance and efficiency while maintaining a top-tier display and build quality. It's one of the best laptops on the market, plain and simple.

Pros
  • Unrivaled performance and battery life thanks to the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max chips
  • Mini-LED display with 120Hz refresh rate and up to 1,600 nits of brightness
  • Premium build quality with an all-aluminum chassis
Cons
  • It may look a bit boring to some
  • Not everyone wants to use macOS
  • It gets quite expensive
$1999 at Amazon (16 inches)

It's impossible to talk about the best laptops on the market without mentioning the MacBook Pro, especially if we're talking about 16-inch laptops. The latest model is a phenomenal machine for performance and battery life, making it easily the best laptop for creators.

Indeed, the Apple M2 Pro and M2 Max are top-of-the-line processors with 12 CPU cores (eight for performance, four for efficiency) and up to a 39-core GPU that delivers a balance of performance and battery life that you just can't get anywhere. The performance can rival the best laptops in the Windows space, but it's so efficient that battery life is in a whole different class compared to its competitors. You can also get up to 96GB of RAM and an 8TB SSD.

To go along with the powerful internals, there's also an absolutely fantastic screen. It's a 16.2-inch panel, and it comes in a super-sharp 3456x2234 resolution, resulting in an aspect ratio slightly taller than 16:10. The screen has a 120Hz adaptive refresh rate that makes motion and animations look smoother. Plus, it has a Mini-LED display with hundreds of individual dimming zones, and it can reach a maximum brightness of 1,600 nits. The only thing you might not like is the notch in the middle of the screen, which is where you'll find the 1080p webcam that delivers very good image quality.

Design-wise, the MacBook Pro isn't particularly exciting, but it is premium. It's a solid aluminum laptop that comes in either Silver or Space Grey (dark silver), so it looks very subdued. Thanks to the powerful specs, it gets a bit heavy, starting at 4.7 pounds of weight. Finally, in terms of ports, the latest MacBook Pro models come with three Thunderbolt 4 ports, along with HDMI, a headphone jack, a full-size SD card reader, and a MagSafe connector for charging. It's worth noting that the Thunderbolt ports don't support things like external GPUs here, but otherwise, it's a solid setup.

This is undoubtedly one of the best laptops on the market, and while it's pricey, we couldn't not recommend it.

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

Subdued design and multiple configurations available

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is a refreshed version of the ThinkPad X1. It now features the more powerful 13th-gen Intel processors, while keeping the class ThinkPad Design we all know and love. It's one of the most powerful business laptops on the market, and it delivers the reliability Lenovo has become known for.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel U- or P-series processors
  • Subdued design with a lot of ports
  • 5MP webcam
Cons
  • Somewhat heavy
  • Expensive, especially for build-to-order configurations
$1391 at Lenovo $2050 at Best Buy

If you need a laptop for work, and you want one with a 16-inch display to get more done faster, the ThinkPad X1 Carbon is a great option that checks all the boxes without being overly fancy.

At a performance level, you can choose 13th-generation Intel processors from either the P-series or the U-series. The P-series models go up to a Core i7-1370P with 14 cores and 20 threads, while the U-series maxes out with a Core i7-1365U with 10 cores and 12 threads. P-series processors can be faster, while the U series is better for battery life. Additionally, you can get up to 64GB of RAM (user-removable) and a 2TB PCIe 4.0 SSD.

You'll get a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio, and since this is a laptop focused on nailing the basics, the entry-level configuration comes in Full HD+ resolution and 400 nits of brightness. However, you can also opt for a 2.8K OLED panel for a stunning visual experience with 500 nits (HDR) of peak brightness. Above that display is also a 5MP webcam with optional Windows Hello support.

For a business laptop, the Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon is exactly what you'd expect. It features the design we all know and love from the ThinkPad, with a carbon fiber top and an aluminum bottom. Starting at 2.4 pounds, it's a comfortable laptop to take with you to the office and beyond. It also features great connectivity, with two Thunderbolt 4 ports, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and even an optional Smart Card reader.

Acer Chromebook 516 GE
Best Chromebook

Fast performance and a sharp display for ChromeOS

$449 $649 Save $200

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE is marketed as a gaming laptop, but it's just a great laptop for anyone. With 12th-generation Intel Core processors and a sharp display, it's perfect for all kinds of users, plus it has some personality with the RGB keyboard.

Pros
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • RGB keyboard adds some personality
Cons
  • Windows laptops have newer processors now
  • No biometric authentication
$530 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy

There isn't a huge market for 16-inch Chromebooks out there, but that shouldn't take away from how good the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is. If you want the best ChromeOS laptop on the market, this should be on your mind regardless of size.

Powering the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is a 12th-generation Intel Core i5-1240P processor with 12 cores and 16 threads. It's not the newest processor around, but it's still very fast, especially on ChromeOS. It comes with a pretty modest 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD for storage. It may be advertised for gaming, but those are specs you'd find in any ultrabook, which may actually be a good thing since it makes this a great machine for anyone.

What makes it even better is the 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio and sharp Quad HD+ resolution. That creates a great viewing experience, and it's excellent for getting work done. It even has a 120Hz refresh rate (where some of that gaming DNA comes in), so animations and motion look much smoother. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, though there's no biometric authentication available there.

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE doesn't try to be too fancy in terms of looks, with a simple black design, but it does have an RGB keyboard which adds a bit of flair if you're into that. At 3.75 pounds, it's not exactly light, but it's reasonable for the size. Ports are pretty good, too, with two USB Type-C and one Type-A port, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack.

As far as Chromebooks go, it doesn't get much better than the Chromebook 516 GE, so if you want one in the 16-inch size, you can't go wrong here.

Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
Best gaming laptop

Monster performance in a premium design

$2049 $2749 Save $700

The Lenovo Legion Pro 7i is a beastly gaming laptop with top-notch performance. It has incredibly powerful Intel processors and Nvidia graphics, along with a great display for gaming. It even has a premium metal design.

Pros
  • Up to a 24-core processor and Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics
  • Quad HD+ display with super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate
  • Lots of ports
Cons
  • No Windows Hello support
  • Heavy
  • Expensive
$2990 at Lenovo $2049 at B&H

Gaming is one of the most popular hobbies in the world, and the 16-inch form factor is arguably ideal for it. If you want the best option to support that activity, the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i should definitely be on your radar.

Performance is one thing that this laptop certainly isn't missing, with up to a 24-core, 32-thread Intel Core i9-13900HX delivering absolutely outstanding performance for a laptop. That, combined with up to an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4090 Laptop GPU, gives you all the performance you would want to run your games at high settings and high frame rates. You can also configure it with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD to store all of your games.

You're getting a great display here too: a 16-inch panel with a 16:10 aspect ratio and super-sharp Quad HD+ resolution, making it easy to get some work done too. And with the smooth 240Hz refresh rate, gaming will be as enjoyable as it gets. Most games probably can't hit that maximum resolution and refresh rate, but for lighter titles, this is fantastic. There's also a 1080p webcam above the display, which is nice to see, although there's no Windows Hello support (not even through a fingerprint reader).

The Legion Pro 7i also impresses in terms of design, since it's a mostly metal chassis that looks and feels very premium. Combined with the high-end specs, this makes for a very heavy laptop, starting at 6.17 pounds, but that makes sense with something like this. And in terms of ports, we're looking at two USB-C ports (one with Thunderbolt 4), four USB Type-A ports, HDMI, RJ45 Ethernet, and a headphone jack, so there's no shortage of connectivity options.

There's a lot to love in the Lenovo Legion Pro 7i, and if you can afford it, it's one of the very best gaming laptops on the market.

LG Gram 16 (2023)
Best lightweight laptop

Fast performance in a portable package

The LG Gram 16 is a lightweight and powerful 16-inch laptop that features a crisp display, solid battery life, and a very sleek design. Featuring Intel's 13th-generation CPUs, there's enough power for this to be your ultra portable work machine.

Pros
  • Incredibly sleek design
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display
  • Just 2.6 pounds
Cons
  • Lack of a touchscreen option
$1030 at Amazon

Bigger laptops do tend to be heavier (naturally), but if you want a machine that has a bigger screen, impressive performance, and is also super light, then the LG Gram 16 is for you.

First off, in terms of performance, the LG Gram 16 comes with up to a 13th-generation Intel Core i7, with 12 cores and 16 threads, able to handle day-to-day tasks without a hitch. You can choose between 16GB or 32GB of RAM, and you also get a 1TB SSD. This leaves no room for poor performance here, which explains the higher price tag.

By default, the LG Gram 16 comes with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics, but you have the option of upgrading to a model with a discrete Nvidia RTX 3050 for an extra boost to the graphics performance. Adding to that is a crisp IPS panel that delivers Quad HD+ resolution. The configuration with the discrete RTX 3050 will offer a variable refresh rate of up to 144Hz, but the standard Gram 16 with integrated Intel Iris Xe graphics comes with a standard 60Hz IPS panel. You also get a 1080p webcam with Windows Hello facial recognition support. The best part: all of these things are included by default.

Finally, there's the design, with the first highlight being the fact that this 16-inch laptop starts at just 2.62 pounds for the configuration with integrated graphics. The dedicated GPU factors into the weight though, bringing the total weight of the LG Gram 16 to 2.86 pounds from 2.62 pounds. There are slightly lighter 16-inch laptops out there, but this one makes up for it in performance capacity. As for ports, you get two Thunderbolt 4 connections, USB Type-A, a headphone jack, and a microSD card reader, so it's a solid setup.

Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
Best budget laptop

A solid experience on the cheap

$550 $750 Save $200

The Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a relatively basic laptop, but it covers the basics with solid performance and it has a 16-inch display with a 16:10 aspect ratio. Plus, since it's a convertible, it supports touch and pen input, too.

Pros
  • Modern AMD Ryzen 7000 series processors
  • Touchscreen with pen support and a 16:10 aspect ratio
  • 1080p webcam
Cons
  • Fairly heavy
  • Display doesn't have the best colors
$550 at Lenovo

Not everyone wants to spend thousands of dollars on a laptop, but the 16-inch form factor does tend to be reserved for more premium products. Thankfully, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 convertible is an exception, which does enough to offer you a good experience for day-to-day use.

The base model of the IdeaPad Flex 5 comes with an AMD Ryzen 5 7530U, a solid processor with 6 cores and 12 threads, which should be more than enough for web browsing, watching movies, and creating documents or presentations. It starts at 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD at minimum, with some upgrade options if you want to spend a little more.

Having a 16-inch display in this price range is fairly rare, but it's great to see one with a 16:10 aspect ratio and Full HD+ resolution, even if the colors won't look quite as amazing as the others here. Plus, since this is a convertible, you can use touch input and even a pen (included in some models) to interact with it more naturally. To top it off, there's even a 1080p webcam, which lacks Windows Hello support, but you can use the fingerprint reader for that instead.

Design is one area that tends to be lacking in budget laptops, and here, it kind of shows. This one uses a mix of aluminum and plastic for the build, which makes for a heavy laptop starting at 4.63 pounds. Again, not unexpected for something in this price range. It does come in two color options, though both are fairly subdued. As for ports, one USB-C connection, two USB Type-A ports, HDMI, a headphone jack, and a full-size SD card reader make for a pretty good experience overall.

With the regular price starting at $700, the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 is a great laptop with a versatile form factor at a very reasonable price. If you want to save money in this category, this is the way to go.

Best 16-inch laptops in 2023: Bottom line

These options cover a lot of use cases and there's something for everyone here. If you want the best overall experience, the Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is hard to beat. It has fast processors, a fantastic display, a premium design, and even a solid supply of ports that beats many competitors in the same space. It's a huge leap forward for the series too, and it's good to see Samsung invest heavily in making a premium device.

Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall
$1663 $1750 Save $87

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

$1450 at Samsung $1663 at Best Buy

If you aren't looking for something expensive, you can still have a great time with the Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5. It doesn't have the amazing display or the same level of performance, but for day-to-day use at home or at school, it will meet your needs. It has a large screen, complete with touch support, and modern AMD processors that should deliver strong performance overall.
item.content:encoded:
For many years, 15-inch laptops were some of the most popular on the market since they offered a nice balance of portability and screen real estate. But in recent years, you may have noticed a trend where more and more 16-inch laptops replace 15-inch models. Many of the best laptops you can buy today have 16-inch screens, and fewer have 15-inch displays.

The reason for that is simple — we're not just making screens bigger. In most cases, 16-inch laptops come in a 15-inch form factor, and that's because companies are moving away from the classic 16:9 aspect ratio that became the standard in the mid-2000s. Instead, we now have a taller 16:10 format, which is far better for productivity. The taller screen means you get more vertical space to hold more lines of text, for a better view of the video and audio tracks in a video editing interface, and to more easily work with all the clutch tools in a photo editor like Photoshop.

There are lots of great reasons to get a 16-inch laptop compared to a 15-inch one, so we're here to help you choose the best one for yourself. There's no shortage of solid options on the market at this point, so let's dive right in.

  • Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
    Best overall
    $1450 at Samsung
  • Source: HP
    HP Spectre x360 16 (2024)
    Best convertible
    $1300 at HP
  • Apple MacBook Pro (M2, 2023)
    Best creator laptop
    $1999 at Amazon (16 inches)
  • Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
    Best business laptop
    $1391 at Lenovo
  • Acer Chromebook 516 GE
    Best Chromebook
    $449 at Best Buy
  • Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
    Best gaming laptop
    $2049 at B&H
  • LG Gram 16 (2023)
    Best lightweight laptop
    $1030 at Amazon
  • Source: Lenovo
    Lenovo IdeaPad Flex 5 (16-inch, 2023)
    Best budget laptop
    $550 at Lenovo
Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro
Best overall

Fast performance, stunning display, and premium build

$1663 $1750 Save $87

The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is a premium aluminum laptop with 13th-generation Intel Core processors and a stunning AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It also has a solid selection of ports.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel Core processors deliver great performance
  • Beautiful Dynamic AMOLED 2X display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Good supply of ports
Cons
  • HDMI output limited to 4K at 30Hz
  • No Windows Hello facial recognition
$1450 at Samsung $1663 at Best Buy

Samsung has been making some pretty great laptops over the past few years, then in 2023, it became one of the best in the industry with the Galaxy Book 3 Pro.

In terms of performance, we're looking at 13th-generation Intel processors, up to a Core i7-1260P with 12 cores and 16 threads, which deliver great performance for day-to-day tasks. It's a mild but welcome upgrade over the previous generation, and with 16GB of RAM and up to a 1TB SSD, you are equipped for a great experience.

The big upgrade is the display. The Samsung Galaxy Book 3 Pro is the first in the lineup to come in a 16-inch size thanks to switching to the 16:10 aspect ratio. The resolution is much sharper, too, at 2880x1800, and it even has a smooth 120Hz refresh rate now. It's simply better in every way. The webcam has the same 1080p sensor, and although there's no facial recognition, you can use Windows Hello with the fingerprint reader on the power button.

Another notable upgrade is the design, with Samsung switching to an aluminum chassis that feels much more premium and sturdy. It looks sleek and subdued in both Graphite and Beige colorways, too. Unfortunately, aluminum is much heavier than magnesium, so this laptop comes in at 3.44 pounds, even though it's still very thin. It's still portable, but significantly heavier than its predecessor. However, the port selection here is great, including two Thunderbolt 4 ports, one USB Type-A, HDMI, a 3.5mm headphone jack, and a microSD card reader.

Previous Samsung Galaxy laptops were already great, but the changes made to the Galaxy Book 3 Pro make this one of the best laptops on the market.

HP Spectre x360 16 (2024)
Best convertible
$1300 $1600 Save $300

The 2024 HP Spectre x360 16 comes with powerful Intel processors and optional Nvidia RTX 4050 graphics for workloads that require more GPU power. It has a super-sharp 16-inch display, including an optional OLED panel, too.

Pros
  • Intel Core Ultra chip
  • OLED panel option
  • Dedicated GPU available
Cons
  • Relatively bulky
$1300 at HP $1750 at Best Buy

Clamshell laptops are fine, but if you're like me, nothing beats the versatility of a convertible. If you're looking for a large one, the HP Spectre x360 16 is your best bet.

The latest model of the HP Spectre x360 16 comes with an Intel Core Ultra 7 155H chip featuring 16 cores and 22 threads. Graphics-wise, you can choose between Intel's Arc integrated solution or opt for an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050. Naturally, the 4050 has more performance than the integrated Arc graphics, which will be the obvious pick for creators that need more GPU power. You can also get this laptop with up to 32GB of RAM and a 2TB SSD.

2:25
Related
HP Spectre x360 16 (2024) review: Major changes pay off for HP's premium convertible
A 9MP webcam, improved keyboard, new speaker setup, and massive haptic touchpad are just some of the standout features.

When it comes to display, you can choose between a 2560x1600 120Hz IPS panel or a 2.8K 120Hz OLED panel. Thanks to the more premium panel tech with OLED, you will get richer image quality as well as a higher peak brightness of 500 nits vs the IPS panel's 400 nits. You're also getting a truly impressive 9MP webcam with infared capability, human presence detection, Windows hello, and privacy alerts.

In terms of looks, the Spectre x360 16 comes in a unique dual-tone color scheme, mixing black with brass accents, or dark blue with light blue accents. Being a large convertible, it's a fairly heavy laptop, coming in at 4.3 pounds. Still, it's decently portable. As for the ports, HP gives you two Thunderbolt 4 connections, one USB Type-A port, HDMI, and a headphone jack. It's a pretty solid setup for a modern laptop.

The HP Spectre x360 16 can be a bit pricey, but it's a fantastic laptop in just about every way.

Terrific performance and battery life

$1999 $2499 Save $500

$1999 at Amazon (16 inches)

Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11
Best business laptop

Subdued design and multiple configurations available

The Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 11 is a refreshed version of the ThinkPad X1. It now features the more powerful 13th-gen Intel processors, while keeping the class ThinkPad Design we all know and love. It's one of the most powerful business laptops on the market, and it delivers the reliability Lenovo has become known for.

Pros
  • 13th-gen Intel U- or P-series processors
  • Subdued design with a lot of ports
  • 5MP webcam
Cons

  • Expensive, especially for build-to-order configurations
$1391 at Lenovo $2050 at Best Buy

If you need a laptop for work, and you want one with a 16-inch display to get more done faster, the ThinkPad X1 Carbon is a great option that checks all the boxes without being overly fancy.

Acer Chromebook 516 GE
Best Chromebook

Fast performance and a sharp display for ChromeOS

$449 $649 Save $200

The Acer Chromebook 516 GE is marketed as a gaming laptop, but it's just a great laptop for anyone. With 12th-generation Intel Core processors and a sharp display, it's perfect for all kinds of users, plus it has some personality with the RGB keyboard.

Pros
  • Sharp Quad HD+ display with 120Hz refresh rate
  • Fast 12th-gen Intel processors
  • RGB keyboard adds some personality
Cons
  • Windows laptops have newer processors now
  • No biometric authentication
$530 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy

There isn't a huge market for 16-inch Chromebooks out there, but that shouldn't take away from how good the Acer Chromebook 516 GE is. If you want the best ChromeOS laptop on the market, this should be on your mind regardless of size.

Lenovo Legion Pro 7i Gen 8
Best gaming laptop

Monster performance in a premium design

$2049 $2749 Save $700

Pros
  • Up to a 24-core processor and Nvidia RTX 4090 graphics
  • Quad HD+ display with super-smooth 240Hz refresh rate
  • Lots of ports
Cons
  • No Windows Hello support
  • Heavy
  • Expensive
$2990 at Lenovo $2049 at B&H
